task_completion.description: Verify the channel is created with the correct name
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a channel named exactly 'Patient_Notification_API' in the NextGen Connect Administrator Channels list.

task_completion.description: Verify the source is configured as a TCP Listener on the correct port
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Source tab for the channel. The Connector Type should be 'TCP Listener' and the Port should be '6661'.

task_completion.description: Verify the destination is configured as an HTTP Sender with the correct URL and Content-Type
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Destinations tab. The Connector Type should be 'HTTP Sender'. The URL must be 'http://webhook-server:8888/api/patient-notification' and the Content-Type (or headers) should specify 'application/json'.

task_completion.description: Verify the JSON payload structure and HL7 field mappings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Destination Template or Transformer scripts. There should be a JSON structure containing the keys 'mrn', 'firstName', 'lastName', 'dateOfBirth', and 'eventType'. These should be mapped to HL7 fields (e.g., PID.3.1, PID.5.2, PID.5.1, PID.7.1, MSH.9.2) or corresponding variables.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel is deployed and in a STARTED state
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Dashboard view in NextGen Connect. The 'Patient_Notification_API' channel should be listed with a green 'Started' status.

task_completion.description: Verify the channel successfully processed the sample HL7 message
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for message statistics on the Dashboard showing Received and Sent counts > 0 for this channel, OR a terminal/log window showing the webhook server received the JSON payload with the expected values (e.g., PAT78432, ELENA).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI to configure the channel
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Java-based NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) Administrator interface, not just API calls via curl or a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not hardcode the expected JSON values
integrity.visual_evidence: In the payload template or transformer, the agent must use variables or HL7 path references (like msg['PID']...) rather than typing the literal strings 'PAT78432', 'ELENA', 'MARTINEZ', etc.

integrity.description: Verify the channel is actually deployed in the engine
integrity.visual_evidence: The Dashboard should show the actual engine status with the channel active, rather than just a saved draft in the Channels view.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Channel Name: 'Patient_Notification_API'. Webhook URL: 'http://webhook-server:8888/api/patient-notification'. If the agent tests the channel using the sample HL7 message, the expected JSON payload sent to the webhook should contain: mrn='PAT78432', firstName='ELENA', lastName='MARTINEZ', dateOfBirth='19780622', and eventType='A01'.
